description: Maps EU AI Act articles to AumOS protocol sections and compliance-mapper controls

articleMappings.title: Prohibited AI Practices
articleMappings.summary: Lists AI systems that are entirely prohibited within the EU.
articleMappings.remediationNote: Systems matching Article 5 criteria must not be deployed. No AumOS tool can remediate a prohibited use case.

articleMappings.title: Classification Rules for High-Risk AI Systems
articleMappings.summary: Establishes rules for classifying AI systems as high-risk under Annex II or Annex III.
articleMappings.remediationNote: Use compliance-mapper EUAIActRiskClassifier to document and evidence your risk classification decision.

articleMappings.title: Risk Management System
articleMappings.summary: Requires a documented, iterative risk management system throughout the AI system lifecycle.
articleMappings.remediationNote: Use compliance-mapper for risk management documentation. Maintain a live risk register and misuse scenario log.

articleMappings.title: Data and Data Governance
articleMappings.summary: Training, validation, and testing data must meet quality criteria and be governed appropriately.
articleMappings.remediationNote: Use context-firewall for data domain isolation. Document training data lineage and bias assessments.

articleMappings.title: Technical Documentation
articleMappings.summary: High-risk AI systems must have technical documentation drawn up before being placed on the market.
articleMappings.remediationNote: Use agents-md-spec and aumos-docs templates to generate and maintain technical documentation.

articleMappings.title: Record-Keeping
articleMappings.summary: High-risk AI systems must have automatic logging capabilities enabling record-keeping.
articleMappings.remediationNote: Use agent-audit-trail for comprehensive immutable record keeping of all AI system decisions.

articleMappings.title: Transparency and Provision of Information to Deployers
articleMappings.summary: High-risk AI systems must be sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret outputs.
articleMappings.remediationNote: Use agents-md-spec for agent capability and limitation disclosure. Publish Instructions for Use.

articleMappings.title: Human Oversight
articleMappings.summary: High-risk AI systems must be designed and developed to allow effective human oversight.
articleMappings.remediationNote: Use trust-ladder L0-L3 levels to enforce human oversight requirements appropriate to your risk level.

articleMappings.title: Accuracy, Robustness and Cybersecurity
articleMappings.summary: High-risk AI systems must achieve appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity.
articleMappings.remediationNote: Use anomaly-sentinel plus trust-test for robustness and accuracy validation pipelines.

articleMappings.title: Transparency Obligations for Certain AI Systems
articleMappings.summary: Providers of chatbots, deepfake tools, and AI-generated content systems must disclose AI involvement.
articleMappings.remediationNote: Add AI disclosure using agents-md-spec or explicit UI notification. Label AI-generated content per Article 50.

articleMappings.title: Codes of Practice for General-Purpose AI Models
articleMappings.summary: Providers of minimal-risk AI systems are encouraged to voluntarily adopt codes of conduct.
articleMappings.remediationNote: Consider voluntary adoption of AumOS governance tools and best practices for competitive advantage.

compliance-mapper.description: Compliance evidence generation and risk classification

agents-md-spec.description: Agent capability and transparency disclosure specification

agent-audit-trail.description: Immutable audit logging for AI system decisions

trust-ladder.description: Human oversight levels from fully-manual to supervised-autonomous

anomaly-sentinel.description: Robustness and accuracy monitoring for deployed AI systems

trust-test.description: Accuracy and reliability testing for AI systems
